channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources worldwide

item.title: 🛢️ UAE OPEC'ten Ayrıldı: Küresel Petrol Düzeni Yeniden Yazılıyor
item.description: BAE'nin OPEC'ten ani ayrılışı, kartelin zayıflamasına ve küresel petrol fiyatları üzerinde daha fazla oynaklığa neden olabilir. ABD'ye daha yakınlaşan BAE, kendi üretim artışını serbest bırakırken OPEC'in fiyat belirleme gücünü azaltıyor.

item.title: ⚔️ ABD Ordusu 'Hackathon' ile Silah Sistemlerini Entegre Ediyor
item.description: ABD Ordusu, Lockheed Martin, Boeing, Palantir ve Anduril gibi savunma yüklenicilerini bir araya getirerek silah sistemlerinin birbiriyle konuşmasını sağlamayı amaçlıyor. Ukrayna'dan alınan dersler, hızlı entegrasyonun savaş alanında kritik olduğunu gösteriyor.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Tayvan'ın Güney Çin Denizi Ziyareti: Çin'in 'Gri Bölge' Oyunlarından Biri Daha
item.description: Tayvan Sahil Güvenlik Bakanı'nın Tartışmalı Spratly Adaları'ndaki Taiping (Itu Aba) ziyareti, Çin'in kendi kıyı güvenlik taktiklerini andırıyor. Tayvan, askeri olmayan birimlerle egemenlik iddiasını sürdürürken gerilimi artırmaktan kaçınıyor.

item.title: 🇬🇧 İngiltere'den Güney Çin Denizi'nde Özgürlük Navigasyonu
item.description: Kraliyet Donanması'nın HMS Spey gemisi, Çin'in 'on çizgi' iddiasına karşı Spratly Adaları çevresinde özgürlük navigasyonu operasyonu gerçekleştirdi. 2016'daki uluslararası tahkimi yok sayan Çin'in adalarda askeri varlığı sürüyor.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japonya İkinci Dünya Savaşı Sonrası İlk Kez Güney Çin Denizi'nde Savaş Rolü Üstleniyor
item.description: Japonya, 1400 askerle Filipinler'deki Balikatan tatbikatına katılarak savaş rolü üstlenen ilk kez II. Dünya Savaşı sonrası Çin'in kapısında askeri güç projeksiyonu yapıyor. Japonya-Filipinler savunma işbirliği derinleşiyor.

item.title: 🇧🇫📰 Burkina Faso junta secretly detained investigative journalist, Reporters Without Borders says
item.description: Reporters Without Borders reports that Burkina Faso's military junta secretly held journalist Atiana Serge Oulon and up to 40 others in a makeshift detention facility in Ouagadougou, subjecting them to abuse, since June 2024.

item.title: 🌐💼 US, allies propose separate e-commerce duty moratorium as WTO talks deadlocked
item.description: The US and allies plan to proceed with their own moratorium on e-commerce duties if Brazil and Turkey continue blocking extension of the global deal at WTO negotiations. The 1998 moratorium banning digital tariffs faces expiry.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Ukraine says Russia violated Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire with drone and missile attacks
item.description: Ukraine accused Russia of breaking a unilateral ceasefire called by President Zelenskiy, with 108 combat drones and three missiles striking Ukrainian cities. Russia announced its own May 8-9 ceasefire for Victory Day commemorations.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Hosts Iranian Foreign Minister as Beijing Seeks Role in Hormuz Negotiations
item.description: Iranian FM Araghchi met with his Chinese counterpart in Beijing as China positions itself as a key mediator in the US-Iran conflict, with Trump pausing the Hormuz escort operation pending a possible peace deal.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Pauses Hormuz Escort Mission, Citing Progress Toward Iran Peace Deal
item.description: President Trump announced a pause in Operation Freedom to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz, stating that progress is being made toward a comprehensive agreement with Iran as the ceasefire continues to hold.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Intercepts Iranian Missiles and Drones as Attacks Continue
item.description: The UAE Disaster Management Authority confirmed its defense systems successfully intercepted missile and drone attacks from Iran, urging residents to remain in safe locations amid ongoing hostilities.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo marks first year with sharper voice and clashes with Trump
item.description: Pope Leo celebrates his first anniversary on Friday with a higher profile and more outspoken stance on global issues, including criticism of the US-Iran war that has drawn sharp rebukes from President Trump.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Cuts Mobile Internet in Moscow and St. Petersburg Ahead of Victory Day Parade
item.description: Russian authorities shut down mobile internet services in major cities days before the annual May 9 Victory Day celebrations, citing security concerns amid heightened fears of Ukrainian drone attacks on the scaled-back military parade.

item.title: ⚖️ EU Formally Backs Nuremberg-Style Tribunal to Prosecute Russian Leaders for War of Aggression
item.description: EU ministers voted to join a Council of Europe court that will prosecute Russian leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine, the same charge used against Nazi leaders at Nuremberg. The tribunal aims to hold Putin and other top officials accountable.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Approves $373.6 Million JDAM Weapons Sale to Ukraine
item.description: The US State Department approved a weapons sale including extended-range Joint Direct Attack Munition systems to Ukraine. The package includes over 1,500 JDAM tail kits to enhance Ukraine's precision strike capabilities.

item.title: 🔐 Palo Alto Networks Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited to Hack Enterprise Firewalls
item.description: A critical buffer overflow vulnerability (CVE-2026-0300) in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS has been exploited in targeted attacks against enterprise firewalls. The flaw allows unauthenticated remote code execution with root privileges. Patches expected May 13.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Two Russian hypersonic missile scientists convicted of treason, sentenced to 12 years
item.description: Two Russian physicists involved in hypersonic missile research were convicted of treason and sentenced to 12.5 years each in a closed-door trial. The cases are part of a broader crackdown on academics amid heightened wartime security.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea inflation hits near two-year high on Middle East conflict oil surge
item.description: South Korea's consumer prices rose 2.6% in April, the fastest pace in nearly two years, driven by soaring oil prices from the Iran war. The data raises pressure on the central bank to consider rate hikes.

item.title: 🇺🇳 Rubio calls UN Hormuz resolution a test of the body's utility
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged China and Russia not to veto a US-proposed UN resolution demanding Iran stop attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a test of whether the UN remains relevant.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Rubio Claims US Achieved Iran Operation Objectives
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated the US has achieved its objectives in the Iran campaign despite not securing Tehran's enriched uranium, adding that protecting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz is a separate defensive operation. He confirmed 10 civilian sailors have died in the strait during the conflict.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Rubio: Israel-Lebanon Peace Deal Achievable Despite Hezbollah
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said peace between Israel and Lebanon was achievable but identified Hezbollah as a key obstacle. The statement comes amid ongoing regional tensions and separate negotiations involving Israel's northern border.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Aims to Raise $295M from Privatization This Year
item.description: Prime Minister Julia Svyrydenko announced Ukraine plans to raise approximately 13 billion hryvnia ($295 million) from privatizing state assets this year, as Kyiv seeks to strengthen its economy amid ongoing war with Russia.

item.title: 🇦🇪 Gulf states face uncertainty as Iran war reshapes regional alliances
item.description: Middle East expert warns that Gulf monarchies will have mixed feelings about the US after the Iran war, as the conflict exposes growing rift between UAE and Saudi Arabia over energy quotas and regional security.

item.title: 🌏 CICA offers Asian alternative to NATO for Middle East security architecture
item.description: The little-known security forum CICA says it provides architecture for de-escalation in the Middle East, allowing members to project soft power amid Iran war. China leads diplomatic efforts to reduce regional tensions.

item.title: 🇷🇺📱 Russia Cuts Mobile Internet Ahead of Scaled-Back Victory Day Parade
item.description: Russia restricted mobile internet services in Moscow ahead of the annual May 9 parade, which has been scaled down due to the threat of Ukrainian drone attacks.
